attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring or one-time remote agent ("run this every Monday", "open a cleanup PR for X in 2 weeks"), or to manage existing routines. ALSO OFFER PROACTIVELY: after you finish work that has a natural future follow-up, end your reply with a one-line offer to schedule a background agent to do it. Strong signals: a feature flag / gate / experiment / staged rollout was just shipped (offer a one-time agent in ~2 weeks to open a cleanup PR or evaluate results), a new alert/monitor was created (offer a recurring agent to triage it), a TODO/migration with a "remove once X" condition was left behind (offer a one-time agent to do the removal). Skip the offer for refactors, bug fixes, and anything that is done once it ships. Name a concrete action and cadence ("in 2 weeks", "every Monday") and only offer when the run just succeeded — do not pitch a schedule for something that has not happened yet.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- skillserver: Bridge between Claude Code and the MCP skillserver on pylon:4100. Use when the user says "/skillserver", "list skills", "list my skills", "search skills", "show skill", "run skill", "skillserver status", "what skills do I have", or invokes a skill by name that isn't locally installed (e.g. "/skillserver jason", "/skillserver good-morning"). Claude Code does not auto-discover skills hosted on the MCP skillserver — this skill bridges the gap. Do NOT use to create or edit skills (write files directly to `~/projects/configs/skills/` instead).
- superpowers:execute-plan: Deprecated - use the superpowers:executing-plans skill instead
- superpowers:write-plan: Deprecated - use the superpowers:writing-plans skill instead
- superpowers:brainstorm: Deprecated - use the superpowers:brainstorming skill instead
- rust-skills:cache-clean: Clean Rust docs cache
- rust-skills:cache-status: Show Rust docs cache status
- rust-skills:create-llms-for-skills: Generate comprehensive llms.txt from URLs using agent-browser
- rust-skills:update-crate-skill: Update a specific crate skill to latest version
- rust-skills:create-skills-via-llms: Create high-quality Rust crate skills from llms.txt
- rust-skills:docs: Fetch Rust API documentation from docs.rs
- plugin-dev:create-plugin: Guided end-to-end plugin creation workflow with component design, implementation, and validation
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create a scheduled task that can be run on demand or automatically on an interval.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- superpowers:requesting-code-review: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements
- superpowers:subagent-driven-development: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session
- superpowers:using-git-worktrees: Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace or before executing implementation plans - creates isolated git worktrees with smart directory selection and safety verification
- superpowers:writing-plans: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code
- superpowers:using-superpowers: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions
- superpowers:systematic-debugging: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes
- superpowers:receiving-code-review: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable - requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation
- superpowers:verification-before-completion: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always
- superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch: Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work - guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup
- superpowers:dispatching-parallel-agents: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies
- superpowers:brainstorming: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.
- superpowers:writing-skills: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment
- superpowers:test-driven-development: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code
- superpowers:executing-plans: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /home/noah/.claude/skills/skillserver

# Skillserver Bridge

Interact with the MCP skillserver (served over LiteLLM gateway on `pylon:4100`)
to list, search, and invoke skills hosted there. Works from any project —
requires only the `litellm-gateway` MCP server to be connected.

## Setup: load the MCP tools

The skillserver MCP tools are deferred. Before doing anything else, load them:

```
ToolSearch query: "+skillserver"
```

This exposes:

- `mcp__litellm-gateway__skillserver-list_skills`
- `mcp__litellm-gateway__skillserver-read_skill`
- `mcp__litellm-gateway__skillserver-search_skills`
- `mcp__litellm-gateway__skillserver-list_skill_resources`
- `mcp__litellm-gateway__skillserver-read_skill_resource`
- `mcp__litellm-gateway__skillserver-get_skill_resource_info`

## Argument parsing

Parse `jason` and route:

| Argument form             | Action                                         |
| ------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------- |
| (empty) or `list`         | List all skills                                |
| `search <query>`          | Search skills by keyword                       |
| `show <name>`             | Read and display the skill's SKILL.md          |
| `status`                  | Health check                                   |
| `<name>` (any other word) | **Invoke** that skill — read it and follow it  |

The invoke form is the default for a bare name. `/skillserver jason` means
"read the `jason` skill from the MCP server and execute its instructions as
if it had been triggered locally." `/skillserver good-morning` runs the
good-morning routine, etc.

## Actions

### List

```
mcp__litellm-gateway__skillserver-list_skills
```

Format as a table: skill name + one-line description. If the result is long,
group by category or prefix.

### Search

```
mcp__litellm-gateway__skillserver-search_skills(query: "<query>")
```

Show matches with relevance context.

### Show

```
mcp__litellm-gateway__skillserver-read_skill(id: "<name>")
```

Dump the full skill content. Do NOT execute it.

### Invoke

1. Read the skill:
   ```
   mcp__litellm-gateway__skillserver-read_skill(id: "<name>")
   ```
2. If the skill references a `references/` folder or other resources, list
   them and pull what's needed:
   ```
   mcp__litellm-gateway__skillserver-list_skill_resources(id: "<name>")
   mcp__litellm-gateway__skillserver-read_skill_resource(id: "<name>", resource: "<path>")
   ```
3. Follow the skill's instructions as if it had been invoked directly.
   Treat the returned SKILL.md body as authoritative — obey its workflow,
   checkpoints, and constraints exactly.
4. If the skill expects further arguments (e.g. `jason` expects
   `refactor|design|review [target]`), pass through whatever the user
   supplied after the skill name. Example: `/skillserver jason refactor src/foo.ts`
   → invoke `jason` with `refactor src/foo.ts` as its arguments.

### Status

```
mcp__litellm-gateway__skillserver-list_skills
```

If it returns results, report "Skillserver reachable, N skills available."
If it errors, report the error and suggest checking:

- `curl -s http://pylon:4100/health/liveliness`
- Whether the `litellm-gateway` MCP server is connected in `~/.claude.json`

## Error handling

### Skill not found

If `read_skill` returns an error for a name, fall back to:

```
mcp__litellm-gateway__skillserver-search_skills(query: "<name>")
```

Suggest the closest matches.

### MCP server not connected

If `ToolSearch` returns no skillserver tools, the `litellm-gateway` MCP server
isn't attached. Tell the user to check `~/.claude.json` for the
`litellm-gateway` entry and that pylon:4100 is reachable.

### Skill pass-through fails

If a skill's instructions reference local files or env vars that don't exist
in the current project (e.g., `jason` needs a target, `improve` needs a
project to improve), report the mismatch instead of guessing. The skill
definitions are project-agnostic; the *invocation context* may not be.

content.text:
{"content":"# Jason — Senior-Engineer Design Lens

This skill applies a strict design discipline to code. It's derived from direct
mentorship and is meant to be applied aggressively, not as suggestions. The
agent is not optimizing for "code that runs"; it is optimizing for **what the
next developer sees in their IDE when they first try to use this API**.

Language-agnostic. Idioms cited below are C# and TypeScript; translate as
needed (see "Language coverage" at the bottom).

---

## Mode selection

Parse `$ARGUMENTS`:

| Input                          | Mode       | Target                    |
| ------------------------------ | ---------- | ------------------------- |
| `refactor <path-or-symbol>`    | refactor   | existing code to refine   |
| `design <brief>`               | design     | new API or subsystem      |
| `review` (no further args)     | review     | current branch vs. base   |
| `review <ref>`                 | review     | specified diff range      |
| (empty)                        | ask user   | prompt for mode + target  |

After selecting a mode, **read the matching reference file** before starting:

- `refactor` → `references/refactor.md`
- `design`   → `references/design.md`
- `review`   → `references/review.md`

Each reference defines the checkpoint sequence for that mode. The agent must
follow the checkpoints strictly — one checkpoint per turn, with explicit user
approval between each. Do NOT collapse checkpoints.

---

## Core stance

- **Design the call site first.** Before writing any implementation, write the
  ideal call site the consumer would use. Iterate until nothing is awkward.
  The implementation falls out of the call site, not the other way around.
- **Optimize for the IDE experience.** What does autocomplete show? What does
  the compiler error say on the obvious mistake? What can the consumer
  discover by typing a `.`? These are design decisions, not aesthetics.
- **Friction is data.** If something bothers you about the current code, name
  it out loud and fix it. Do not rationalize it.
- **Dead code is debt.** If you're 70–80% sure something will stay dead,
  delete it. Git is the archive.

---

## The nine moves

Apply on every pass. When you refuse to apply one, state why explicitly.

### 1. Call-site-first design
Before any implementation work, write 2–3 candidate call sites. Name what's
awkward about each. Pick the one that requires the least knowledge of
internals. Only then write the implementation that makes that call site legal.
If you produce the first plausible call site and stop, you have failed this
move.

### 2. Name the implicit concept
List every group of values that travel together through three or more
functions without a shared type. Each one is a concept waiting for a name.
Give it a class or type. Example: "plugin + its context" — two parameters
that always appeared together became `CopilotPlugin<TContext>`, and suddenly
the compiler could help.

### 3. Kill ceremonial interfaces and service wrappers
For each interface: is there more than one real implementation with genuinely
different behavior? If not, consider an abstract base class that carries
plumbing *for* authors (e.g., stores the context so subclasses just access
`this.Context`). For each `FooService` class: does it do more than hold
dependencies and forward to `Foo`? If not, inline it. A service that's a
"glorified constructor" is ceremony.

### 4. Dependency direction — framework at the bottom
Draw the module graph. Identify the framework (the code other code builds on
top of). The framework must not depend on its consumers. If `Copilot`
references `GeminiService` directly, that's inverted — `Gemini` should
register itself with `Copilot`, not the reverse. Use keyed DI, registration
callbacks, or extension methods to flip the arrow. A `switch` on an enum that
imports every concrete implementation is a signal this is wrong.

### 5. Canonical internal representation, convert at edges
For each type that crosses a boundary: is this our internal representation,
or is it shaped like one external system's wire format? If the latter,
introduce a clean internal type (usually an enum for choices, separate fields
for compound values) and convert to the vendor's format only at the boundary.
"Aspect ratio as `'16:9'` string" fails this; `AspectRatio.Widescreen` with
conversion in the Gemini adapter passes it.

### 6. Trace every declared dependency
For each dependency a class declares: follow it through the code. Is it
actually used, or is it declared to satisfy a type signature that could
itself be simplified? The `Kernel` that was built, passed in, and then
ignored by every implementation is the canonical failure. Remove unused
dependencies; simplify the signatures that required them.

### 7. Make wrong uses unrepresentable; fail loudly when you can't
- Prefer generics, discriminated unions, and exhaustive switches that the
  compiler can check.
- When the type system can't enforce it, throw. Specifically: `switch` on an
  enum should throw `InvalidOperationException` in the default branch. C#
  enums accept out-of-range casts silently; TypeScript's `never` type catches
  this at compile time — use it. The goal is that a new enum value cannot
  reach production without every handler being updated.
- Shrink public surface. Prefer `internal` (C#) or narrow exports (TS) over
  `public`. Every public symbol is a contract.

### 8. Opinionated implementation, flexible seams
Speculative generality *inside* a module is bad: if there's only one caller,
hard-code it. Speculative generality *at seams where third parties plug in*
is good: make extension-point methods `async` from day one even if current
impls are sync, because changing a sync method to async later breaks every
implementer. The test: "if this changes, who has to change with me?" If the
answer is "only me," hard-code; if it's "every plugin author," design the
seam for change.

### 9. Immutability, and no redundant round-trips
- Collections exposed on public or semi-public types should be
  `ImmutableArray<T>` / `IReadOnlyList<T>` / `ReadonlyArray<T>` unless
  mutation is a real requirement. The performance cost is negligible; the
  peace of mind and cache-safety are not.
- For every value: trace it from entry to exit. If it's parsed into a
  structured form and then re-serialized to its original form without the
  structured form being used, delete the round-trip.

---

## Naming rules

- **Pluralize namespaces**, even for single-item namespaces. `FormFields.Plugin`
  avoids IntelliSense confusion when a class happens to be called `FormField`
  or `Plugin`. `FormField.Plugin` does not.
- **Pick names a consumer would discover.** `plugins.AddFormField(context)`
  discovers itself via autocomplete;
  `new CopilotPluginWrapper<FormFieldPluginContext>(new FormFieldPlugin(), context)`
  does not.
- **If a name forces the caller to know internals, the name is wrong** —
  usually the design is wrong too.

---

## Meta-rule: the itch

After every change, state the single thing about the current state that most
bothers you. If you can name something, there's another move. Stop only when
you genuinely cannot name anything that's still awkward. "Good enough" is not
a stopping condition in strict mode.

---

## Rules that apply to every mode

- **Do not collapse checkpoints.** Each one is a separate turn. If you find
  yourself wanting to skip ahead because "the answer is obvious," that is
  exactly when the checkpoint is most valuable.
- **Do not soften findings.** This is strict mode. If something is wrong, say
  it is wrong. Do not pad with "this is fine but could be better." Either it
  passes a move or it doesn't.
- **Cite the move.** Every proposed change must reference which of the nine
  moves (or which naming rule) justifies it. If you can't cite one, the
  change is aesthetic and should be flagged as such.
- **When you refuse a move, explain why.** Some moves don't apply to every
  situation. If you're not applying one, say which one and why in the
  response for that checkpoint.
- **Surface the itch, always.** After any substantive change, state the single
  thing that still bothers you. Do not stop until you genuinely cannot name
  anything.

---

## Language coverage

Principles are language-agnostic. Translate idioms as needed:

- `internal` → module-private exports (TS `_` prefix, Python `_` prefix,
  Rust `pub(crate)`)
- `ImmutableArray<T>` → `ReadonlyArray<T>` (TS), `frozen dataclass` (Py),
  `Arc<[T]>` (Rust)
- C# generics with `where T : class` → TS generics with `extends`
  constraints, Py `TypeVar` with `bound=`
- Exhaustive switch via `InvalidOperationException` → TS `never` type,
  Rust `match`, Py `match` + `NoMatch` raise

---

## Tuning strictness

Calibrated for learning and for greenfield or actively-refactored code. For a
large legacy codebase where most of the nine moves would fire on every file,
swap the meta-rule: change "Stop only when you genuinely cannot name
anything" to "Stop after 3 iterations or when remaining issues are minor."
The user may request this explicitly — honor it for the session."}

anything" to "Stop after 3 iterations or when remaining issues are minor."
The user may request this explicitly — honor it for the session."}

{"content":"# Refactor mode — checkpoints

Target: existing code the user wants sharpened against the nine moves.

Each checkpoint is a separate turn. Wait for explicit user approval before
proceeding. Do not collapse checkpoints into one response.

---

## Checkpoint 1 — Inventory

Read the target. Do not propose changes yet. Produce:

1. A one-sentence summary of what the code does.
2. Every group of values that travel together through 3+ functions without a
   shared type (Move 2).
3. Every class whose non-trivial behavior reduces to "holds dependencies and
   forwards to X" (Move 3).
4. A dependency graph of the modules involved, with arrows. Flag any arrows
   that point from a framework to its consumers (Move 4).
5. Every declared dependency that isn't meaningfully used (Move 6).
6. Every value that round-trips through an intermediate representation no one
   reads (Move 9).
7. Every `public` symbol with no cross-module caller (Move 7, surface).
8. Every collection that could be immutable and isn't (Move 9).

**Stop. Wait for approval.**

---

## Checkpoint 2 — Call site

Before proposing any implementation change, produce 2–3 candidate call sites
for the primary API. For each, name what's awkward. Pick one. Justify the
choice against the principles. Show the ideal call-site shape as a code
snippet the user can react to (Move 1).

**Stop. Wait for approval.**

---

## Checkpoint 3 — The itch list

Given the inventory and chosen call site, list every change you would make,
ranked by how much friction it removes. For each, cite which of the nine
moves justifies it. Include the ones you are *unsure* about and explain the
uncertainty. Do not filter for "worth doing" — surface everything.

**Stop. Wait for approval.** The user will mark which to apply.

---

## Checkpoint 4 — Apply

Make the approved changes. After each logical group of edits, state the
single remaining thing that most bothers you and whether you want to keep
going (meta-rule: the itch). If you can still name something awkward, you
are not done.

---

## Checkpoint 5 — Final pass

Re-read the result as if you are a new developer encountering this API for
the first time via autocomplete. What does IntelliSense show? What does the
compile error say when the obvious mistake is made? Report. Fix anything
that fails this test.

input.prompt:
I need to find specific UI components in a Phoenix LiveView umbrella project at /home/noah/projects/readaloud/. The frontend lives under apps/readaloud_web. There is also a apps/readaloud_reader app for the reader/playback domain.

Find and report:

1. The "floating pill" — a persistent floating UI element shown across pages. It's the now-playing or current-chapter pill that follows the user across navigation. Find the LiveView component (or live_component / function component) that renders it. Report the file paths and the relevant module/function names.

2. The "book screen" / "chapter list view" — the page that shows a single book and its list of chapters (where the user picks which chapter to play). Report the file paths, the LiveView module, and the template (.heex) location.

3. The "library view" — for cross-reference, the listing of all books.

4. Any router.ex routes for book screen / library / reader.

5. How chapters track read/unread state — find any `read`, `played`, `progress`, `completed`, `finished` field on a chapter schema or any "mark as read" / "mark as played" function. Specifically look in apps/readaloud_reader/lib and apps/readaloud_audiobook/lib. Report the schema field name and any helper functions.

Don't propose changes. Just give me concrete file paths, module names, and short snippets where relevant. Aim for a tight report I can act on; cite line numbers.

content.text:
I've completed a thorough search of the readaloud Phoenix LiveView umbrella project. Here's my report:

## 1. Floating Pill Component

**File:** `/home/noah/projects/readaloud/apps/readaloud_web/lib/readaloud_web_web/live/reader_live.ex`

- **LiveView:** `ReadaloudWebWeb.ReaderLive` (module definition at line 1)
- **Floating pill HTML:** Lines 243-280 in the render function
- **Element ID:** `id="floating-pill"` (line 245)
- **Hook:** `phx-hook="FloatingPillHook"` (line 246)
- The pill is a fixed, persistent UI showing chapter navigation with prev/next buttons, chapter indicator, and settings (lines 251-279)

## 2. Book Screen / Chapter List View

**File:** `/home/noah/projects/readaloud/apps/readaloud_web/lib/readaloud_web_web/live/book_live.ex`

- **LiveView Module:** `ReadaloudWebWeb.BookLive` (line 1)
- **Template:** Embedded in render function at lines 116-312 (no separate .heex file)
- **Chapter List Rendering:** Lines 269-309 (loops through `@chapters` and displays each with current marker)
- **Key function:** `current?(chapter, progress)` at line 385-386 marks the current chapter with badge

## 3. Library View

**File:** `/home/noah/projects/readaloud/apps/readaloud_web/lib/readaloud_web_web/live/library_live.ex`

- **LiveView Module:** `ReadaloudWebWeb.LibraryLive` (line 1)
- **Template:** Embedded in render function at lines 89-234 (no separate .heex file)
- **Book Grid:** Lines 177-231 displays books with progress badges
- **Progress Tracking:** `build_progress_map/1` function (lines 237-242) and `status_badge/2` (lines 272-290) determine badge display (Done/New/Progress)

## 4. Router Routes

**File:** `/home/noah/projects/readaloud/apps/readaloud_web/lib/readaloud_web_web/router.ex`

```
Route               Module/Handler
/                   LibraryLive (line 21)
/books/:id          BookLive (line 22)
/books/:id/read/:chapter_id   ReaderLive (line 23)
/tasks              TasksLive (line 24)
/books/:id/listen/:chapter_id AudioController :listen_redirect (line 27)
```

## 5. Chapter Read/Played State Tracking

**Schema:** `/home/noah/projects/readaloud/apps/readaloud_reader/lib/readaloud_reader/reading_progress.ex`

- **Schema Table:** `reading_progress`
- **Key Fields:**
  - `current_chapter_id` (line 7) - tracks which chapter is currently being read
  - `scroll_position` (line 8, float, default 0.0) - text scroll position
  - `audio_position_ms` (line 9, integer, default 0) - audio playback position in milliseconds
  - `last_read_at` (line 10, utc_datetime) - timestamp of last read activity
  - `book_id` (line 6) - which book

**No explicit "read" or "played" boolean field exists.** Read/played state is inferred from:
- Presence of a `current_chapter_id` (if set, chapters up to that number are considered read)
- The `LibraryLive` helper `progress_chapter_count/2` (lines 292-303) calculates how many chapters are read by finding the chapter number of `current_chapter_id`

**Helper Functions:**

File: `/home/noah/projects/readaloud/apps/readaloud_reader/lib/readaloud_reader.ex`
- `get_progress(book_id)` (line 6) - retrieves progress for a book
- `upsert_progress(attrs)` (line 14) - creates or updates progress record
- `list_progress_for_books(book_ids)` (line 10) - batch fetch for multiple books

**Progress Update Events:**

In `ReaderLive`, progress is updated on:
- Chapter change (lines 77-80, `upsert_progress` with new `current_chapter_id`)
- Scroll events (lines 150-158, `handle_event("scroll", ...)`)
- Audio position changes (lines 161-169, `handle_event("audio_position", ...)`)

There is no separate schema for marking individual chapters as read—progress is tracked at the book level via `current_chapter_id`.

1 defmodule ReadaloudWebWeb.ReaderLive do 2 use ReadaloudWebWeb, :live_view 3 4 alias ReadaloudWebWeb.ThemeSelector 5 6 @impl true 7 def mount(%{"id" => book_id, "chapter_id" => chapter_id}, _session, socket) do 8 book_id = String.to_integer(book_id) 9 chapter_id = String.to_integer(chapter_id) 10 11 book = ReadaloudLibrary.get_book!(book_id) 12 chapter = ReadaloudLibrary.get_chapter!(chapter_id) 13 chapters = ReadaloudLibrary.list_chapters(book_id) 14 15 content = 16 case ReadaloudLibrary.get_chapter_content(chapter) do 17 {:ok, c} -> c 18 {:error, _} -> nil 19 end 20 21 progress = ReadaloudReader.get_progress(book_id) 22 audio = ReadaloudAudiobook.get_chapter_audio(chapter_id) 23 audio_state = determine_audio_state(chapter_id, audio) 24 25 if connected?(socket) do 26 Phoenix.PubSub.subscribe(ReadaloudWeb.PubSub, "tasks:audiobook:#{book_id}") 27 send(self(), :fetch_models) 28 end 29 30 {:ok, 31 socket 32 |> assign( 33 active_nav: :reader, 34 task_count: 0, 35 book: book, 36 chapter: chapter, 37 chapters: chapters, 38 content: content, 39 progress: progress, 40 audio: audio, 41 audio_state: audio_state, 42 models: [], 43 selected_model: default_model(book, []), 44 selected_voice: default_voice(book, []), 45 show_conflict_modal: false, 46 conflict_chapter: nil, 47 generation_progress: 0, 48 initial_scroll: (progress && progress.scroll_position) || 0.0, 49 initial_position_ms: (progress && progress.audio_position_ms) || 0, 50 page_title: "#{chapter.title || "Chapter #{chapter.number}"} — #{book.title}" 51 )} 52 end 53 54 @impl true 55 def handle_params(params, _uri, socket) do 56 if connected?(socket) do 57 progress = socket.assigns.progress 58 is_internal = params["nav"] == "internal" 59 60 if !is_internal && progress && progress.current_chapter_id && 61 progress.current_chapter_id != socket.assigns.chapter.id do 62 conflict_chapter = 63 Enum.find(socket.assigns.chapters, &(&1.id == progress.current_chapter_id)) 64 65 if conflict_chapter do 66 {:noreply, 67 assign(socket, show_conflict_modal: true, conflict_chapter: conflict_chapter)} 68 else 69 ReadaloudReader.upsert_progress(%{ 70 book_id: socket.assigns.book.id, 71 current_chapter_id: socket.assigns.chapter.id 72 }) 73 74 {:noreply, socket} 75 end 76 else 77 ReadaloudReader.upsert_progress(%{ 78 book_id: socket.assigns.book.id, 79 current_chapter_id: socket.assigns.chapter.id 80 }) 81 82 {:noreply, socket} 83 end 84 else 85 {:noreply, socket} 86 end 87 end 88 89 # -- Audio states: :none, :generating, :ready -- 90 91 defp determine_audio_state(chapter_id, audio) do 92 cond do 93 audio != nil -> :ready 94 has_active_task?(chapter_id) -> :generating 95 true -> :none 96 end 97 end 98 99 # -- Event handlers -- 100 101 @impl true 102 def handle_event("prev_chapter", _params, socket) do 103 case prev_chapter(socket.assigns.chapter, socket.assigns.chapters) do 104 nil -> 105 {:noreply, socket} 106 107 ch -> 108 {:noreply, 109 push_navigate(socket, 110 to: ~p"/books/#{socket.assigns.book.id}/read/#{ch.id}?nav=internal" 111 )} 112 end 113 end 114 115 @impl true 116 def handle_event("next_chapter", _params, socket) do 117 case next_chapter(socket.assigns.chapter, socket.assigns.chapters) do 118 nil -> 119 {:noreply, socket} 120 121 ch -> 122 {:noreply, 123 push_navigate(socket, 124 to: ~p"/books/#{socket.assigns.book.id}/read/#{ch.id}?nav=internal" 125 )} 126 end 127 end 128 129 @impl true 130 def handle_event("generate_audio", _params, socket) do 131 book = socket.assigns.book 132 chapter = socket.assigns.chapter 133 model = socket.assigns.selected_model 134 voice = socket.assigns.selected_voice 135 136 ReadaloudLibrary.update_book(book, %{audio_preferences: %{"model" => model, "voice" => voice}}) 137 138 ReadaloudAudiobook.generate_for_chapter(book.id, chapter.id, model: model, voice: voice) 139 140 {:noreply, assign(socket, audio_state: :generating, generation_progress: 0)} 141 end 142 143 @impl true 144 def handle_event("cancel_generation", _params, socket) do 145 cancel_active_tasks(socket.assigns.chapter.id) 146 {:noreply, assign(socket, audio_state: :none, generation_progress: 0)} 147 end 148 149 @impl true 150 def handle_event("scroll", %{"position" => pos}, socket) do 151 ReadaloudReader.upsert_progress(%{ 152 book_id: socket.assigns.book.id, 153 current_chapter_id: socket.assigns.chapter.id, 154 scroll_position: pos 155 }) 156 157 {:noreply, socket} 158 end 159 160 @impl true 161 def handle_event("audio_position", %{"position_ms" => ms}, socket) do 162 ReadaloudReader.upsert_progress(%{ 163 book_id: socket.assigns.book.id, 164 current_chapter_id: socket.assigns.chapter.id, 165 audio_position_ms: ms 166 }) 167 168 {:noreply, socket} 169 end 170 171 @impl true 172 def handle_event("dismiss_conflict", _params, socket) do 173 ReadaloudReader.upsert_progress(%{ 174 book_id: socket.assigns.book.id, 175 current_chapter_id: socket.assigns.chapter.id 176 }) 177 178 {:noreply, assign(socket, show_conflict_modal: false, conflict_chapter: nil)} 179 end 180 181 @impl true 182 def handle_event("go_to_conflict_chapter", _params, socket) do 183 chapter = socket.assigns.conflict_chapter 184 185 {:noreply, 186 socket 187 |> assign(show_conflict_modal: false, conflict_chapter: nil) 188 |> push_navigate(to: ~p"/books/#{socket.assigns.book.id}/read/#{chapter.id}?nav=internal")} 189 end 190 191 @impl true 192 def handle_event("jump_to_chapter", %{"chapter_id" => chapter_id}, socket) do 193 {:noreply, 194 push_navigate(socket, 195 to: ~p"/books/#{socket.assigns.book.id}/read/#{chapter_id}?nav=internal" 196 )} 197 end 198 199 @impl true 200 def handle_event("select_model", %{"model" => model_id}, socket) do 201 model = Enum.find(socket.assigns.models, &(&1[:id] == model_id)) 202 voice = if model, do: List.first(model[:voices] || []), else: socket.assigns.selected_voice 203 {:noreply, assign(socket, selected_model: model_id, selected_voice: voice)} 204 end 205 206 @impl true 207 def handle_event("select_voice", %{"voice" => voice}, socket) do 208 {:noreply, assign(socket, selected_voice: voice)} 209 end 210 211 # -- Async model fetch -- 212 213 @impl true 214 def handle_info(:fetch_models, socket) do 215 models = fetch_models() 216 217 {:noreply, 218 assign(socket, 219 models: models, 220 selected_model: default_model(socket.assigns.book, models), 221 selected_voice: default_voice(socket.assigns.book, models) 222 )} 223 end 224 225 # -- PubSub handler: audio generation completed -- 226 227 @impl true 228 def handle_info({:task_updated, task}, socket) do 229 if task.chapter_id == socket.assigns.chapter.id and task.status == "completed" do 230 audio = defmodule ReadaloudWebWeb.ReaderLive do use ReadaloudWebWeb, :live_view alias ReadaloudWebWeb.ThemeSelector @impl true def mount(%{"id" => book_id, "chapter_id" => chapter_id}, _session, socket) do book_id = String.to_integer(book_id) chapter_id = String.to_integer(chapter_id) book = ReadaloudLibrary.get_book!(book_id) chapter = ReadaloudLibrary.get_chapter!(chapter_id) chapters = ReadaloudLibrary.list_chapters(book_id) content = case ReadaloudLibrary.get_chapter_content(chapter) do {:ok, c} -> c {:error, _} -> nil end progress = ReadaloudReader.get_progress(book_id) audio = ReadaloudAudiobook.get_chapter_audio(chapter_id) audio_state = determine_audio_state(chapter_id, audio) if connected?(socket) do Phoenix.PubSub.subscribe(ReadaloudWeb.PubSub, "tasks:audiobook:#{book_id}") send(self(), :fetch_models) end {:ok, socket |> assign( active_nav: :reader, task_count: 0, book: book, chapter: chapter, chapters: chapters, content: content, progress: progress, audio: audio, audio_state: audio_state, models: [], selected_model: default_model(book, []), selected_voice: default_voice(book, []), show_conflict_modal: false, conflict_chapter: nil, generation_progress: 0, initial_scroll: (progress && progress.scroll_position) || 0.0, initial_position_ms: (progress && progress.audio_position_ms) || 0, page_title: "#{chapter.title || "Chapter #{chapter.number}"} — #{book.title}" )} end @impl true def handle_params(params, _uri, socket) do if connected?(socket) do progress = socket.assigns.progress is_internal = params["nav"] == "internal" if !is_internal && progress && progress.current_chapter_id && progress.current_chapter_id != socket.assigns.chapter.id do conflict_chapter = Enum.find(socket.assigns.chapters, &(&1.id == progress.current_chapter_id)) if conflict_chapter do {:noreply, assign(socket, show_conflict_modal: true, conflict_chapter: conflict_chapter)} else ReadaloudReader.upsert_progress(%{ book_id: socket.assigns.book.id, current_chapter_id: socket.assigns.chapter.id }) {:noreply, socket} end else ReadaloudReader.upsert_progress(%{ book_id: socket.assigns.book.id, current_chapter_id: socket.assigns.chapter.id }) {:noreply, socket} end else {:noreply, socket} end end # -- Audio states: :none, :generating, :ready -- defp determine_audio_state(chapter_id, audio) do cond do audio != nil -> :ready has_active_task?(chapter_id) -> :generating true -> :none end end # -- Event handlers -- @impl true def handle_event("prev_chapter", _params, socket) do case prev_chapter(socket.assigns.chapter, socket.assigns.chapters) do nil -> {:noreply, socket} ch -> {:noreply, push_navigate(socket, to: ~p"/books/#{socket.assigns.book.id}/read/#{ch.id}?nav=internal" )} end end @impl true def handle_event("next_chapter", _params, socket) do case next_chapter(socket.assigns.chapter, socket.assigns.chapters) do nil -> {:noreply, socket} ch -> {:noreply, push_navigate(socket, to: ~p"/books/#{socket.assigns.book.id}/read/#{ch.id}?nav=internal" )} end end @impl true def handle_event("generate_audio", _params, socket) do book = socket.assigns.book chapter = socket.assigns.chapter model = socket.assigns.selected_model voice = socket.assigns.selected_voice ReadaloudLibrary.update_book(book, %{audio_preferences: %{"model" => model, "voice" => voice}}) ReadaloudAudiobook.generate_for_chapter(book.id, chapter.id, model: model, voice: voice) {:noreply, assign(socket, audio_state: :generating, generation_progress: 0)} end @impl true def handle_event("cancel_generation", _params, socket) do cancel_active_tasks(socket.assigns.chapter.id) {:noreply, assign(socket, audio_state: :none, generation_progress: 0)} end @impl true def handle_event("scroll", %{"position" => pos}, socket) do ReadaloudReader.upsert_progress(%{ book_id: socket.assigns.book.id, current_chapter_id: socket.assigns.chapter.id, scroll_position: pos }) {:noreply, socket} end @impl true def handle_event("audio_position", %{"position_ms" => ms}, socket) do ReadaloudReader.upsert_progress(%{ book_id: socket.assigns.book.id, current_chapter_id: socket.assigns.chapter.id, audio_position_ms: ms }) {:noreply, socket} end @impl true def handle_event("dismiss_conflict", _params, socket) do ReadaloudReader.upsert_progress(%{ book_id: socket.assigns.book.id, current_chapter_id: socket.assigns.chapter.id }) {:noreply, assign(socket, show_conflict_modal: false, conflict_chapter: nil)} end @impl true def handle_event("go_to_conflict_chapter", _params, socket) do chapter = socket.assigns.conflict_chapter {:noreply, socket |> assign(show_conflict_modal: false, conflict_chapter: nil) |> push_navigate(to: ~p"/books/#{socket.assigns.book.id}/read/#{chapter.id}?nav=internal")} end @impl true def handle_event("jump_to_chapter", %{"chapter_id" => chapter_id}, socket) do {:noreply, push_navigate(socket, to: ~p"/books/#{socket.assigns.book.id}/read/#{chapter_id}?nav=internal" )} end @impl true def handle_event("select_model", %{"model" => model_id}, socket) do model = Enum.find(socket.assigns.models, &(&1[:id] == model_id)) voice = if model, do: List.first(model[:voices] || []), else: socket.assigns.selected_voice {:noreply, assign(socket, selected_model: model_id, selected_voice: voice)} end @impl true def handle_event("select_voice", %{"voice" => voice}, socket) do {:noreply, assign(socket, selected_voice: voice)} end # -- Async model fetch -- @impl true def handle_info(:fetch_models, socket) do models = fetch_models() {:noreply, assign(socket, models: models, selected_model: default_model(socket.assigns.book, models), selected_voice: default_voice(socket.assigns.book, models) )} end # -- PubSub handler: audio generation completed -- @impl true def handle_info({:task_updated, task}, socket) do if task.chapter_id == socket.assigns.chapter.id and task.status == "completed" do audio = ReadaloudAudiobook.get_chapter_audio(socket.assigns.chapter.id) {:noreply, assign(socket, audio: audio, audio_state: :ready)} else {:noreply, socket} end end # -- Render -- @impl true def render(assigns) do ~H"""
<%!-- 1. Floating pill (immersive nav) --%>
<.link navigate={~p"/"} class="btn btn-ghost btn-xs btn-circle" title="Library"> <.icon name="hero-home" class="w-4 h-4" />
<%!-- Chapter bar (slide-down from pill) --%>
%{id: c.id, number: c.number, title: c.title} end) ) } data-book-id={@book.id} class="fixed top-14 left-1/2 -translate-x-1/2 z-49 w-[90vw] max-w-xl bg-base-200/90 backdrop-blur-xl rounded-2xl shadow-lg border border-base-content/6 p-3 origin-top transition-all duration-200 scale-y-0 opacity-0 pointer-events-none" > <%!-- Scrubber row --%>
<%!-- Chapter strip --%>
<%!-- Reader settings popover --%>

Reading Settings

<%!-- Auto-next chapter toggle --%>
<%!-- Theme selector --%>
Theme
<%!-- 2. Reading area --%>
<%!-- Loading skeleton --%>
<%!-- Chapter title --%>
{@chapter.title || "Chapter #{@chapter.number}"}
<%!-- Chapter content: with word spans when audio ready, plain HTML otherwise --%>
{raw(prepare_text_with_spans(@content))}
{raw(@content)}
<%!-- Re-sync button (shown when user manually scrolls during playback) --%> <%!-- 3. Bottom bar: three states --%> <%!-- State 1: No audio --%>
<.icon name="hero-speaker-wave" class="w-6 h-6 text-base-content/40" />
Listen to Audiobook
Generate an audiobook version of this chapter
<%!-- State 2: Generating --%>
<.icon name="hero-arrow-path" class="w-6 h-6 animate-spin text-primary" />
Generating Audio...
You can keep reading while this runs
<%!-- State 3: Audio ready — full player --%>
<%!-- Scrubber (hidden when collapsed) --%>
<%!-- Controls row --%>
<%!-- Skip back (hidden when collapsed) --%> <%!-- Play/pause --%> <%!-- Skip forward (hidden when collapsed) --%> <%!-- Collapsed-only mini scrubber --%>
<%!-- Time display --%> 0:00 / 0:00
<%!-- Speed badge (click to cycle) --%> <%!-- Volume slider (hidden when collapsed, hidden on mobile) --%>
<.icon name="hero-speaker-wave" class="w-4 h-4 opacity-50 shrink-0" />
<%!-- Regenerate audio --%> <%!-- Collapse toggle --%>
<%!-- Chapter navigation footer (when no audio bar) --%>
<%= if prev = prev_chapter(@chapter, @chapters) do %> <.link navigate={~p"/books/#{@book.id}/read/#{prev.id}?nav=internal"} class="btn btn-ghost btn-sm" > ← Previous <% else %>
<% end %> <%= if nxt = next_chapter(@chapter, @chapters) do %> <.link navigate={~p"/books/#{@book.id}/read/#{nxt.id}?nav=internal"} class="btn btn-ghost btn-sm" > Next → <% end %>
<%!-- Accidental navigation conflict modal --%>

Continue reading?

Your last position was on {if @conflict_chapter, do: @conflict_chapter.title || "Chapter #{@conflict_chapter.number}", else: "another chapter"} . Would you like to go back there or stay here?

""" end # -- Private helpers -- defp prev_chapter(current, chapters) do idx = Enum.find_index(chapters, &(&1.id == current.id)) if idx && idx > 0, do: Enum.at(chapters, idx - 1), else: nil end defp next_chapter(current, chapters) do idx = Enum.find_index(chapters, &(&1.id == current.id)) if idx && idx < length(chapters) - 1, do: Enum.at(chapters, idx + 1), else: nil end defp chapter_index(chapter, chapters) do Enum.find_index(chapters, &(&1.id == chapter.id)) || 0 end defp has_active_task?(chapter_id) do ReadaloudAudiobook.list_tasks() |> Enum.any?(&(&1.chapter_id == chapter_id && &1.status in ["pending", "processing"])) end defp cancel_active_tasks(chapter_id) do ReadaloudAudiobook.list_tasks() |> Enum.filter(&(&1.chapter_id == chapter_id && &1.status in ["pending", "processing"])) |> Enum.each(fn task -> import Ecto.Query case ReadaloudLibrary.Repo.one( from(j in Oban.Job, where: fragment("?->>'task_id' = ?", j.args, ^to_string(task.id)), where: j.state in ["available", "executing"], limit: 1 ) ) do nil -> :ok job -> Oban.cancel_job(job.id) end end) end # Ported from PlayerLive — wraps each word in a span with data-word-index for highlighting defp prepare_text_with_spans(html) do segments = Regex.split(~r/(<[^>]+>)/, html, include_captures: true) {_idx, io} = Enum.reduce(segments, {0, []}, fn segment, {idx, acc} -> if String.starts_with?(segment, "<") do {idx, [acc, segment]} else # Split em/en-dashes into spaces to match aligner tokenization normalized = segment |> String.replace("\u2014", " ") |> String.replace("\u2013", " ") words = String.split(normalized, ~r/(\s+)/, include_captures: true) {new_idx, parts} = Enum.reduce(words, {idx, []}, fn word, {i, wacc} -> if String.match?(word, ~r/^\s*$/) do {i, [wacc, word]} else span = "#{word}" {i + 1, [wacc, span]} end end) {new_idx, [acc, parts]} end end) IO.iodata_to_binary(io) end end

1 defmodule ReadaloudWebWeb.BookLive do 2 use ReadaloudWebWeb, :live_view 3 4 alias ReadaloudImporter.CoverResolver 5 6 @impl true 7 def mount(%{"id" => id}, _session, socket) do 8 book = ReadaloudLibrary.get_book!(String.to_integer(id)) 9 chapters = ReadaloudLibrary.list_chapters(book.id) 10 progress = ReadaloudReader.get_progress(book.id) 11 audio_map = build_audio_map(chapters, book) 12 models = fetch_models() 13 14 if connected?(socket) do 15 Phoenix.PubSub.subscribe(ReadaloudWeb.PubSub, "tasks:audiobook:#{book.id}") 16 ReadaloudAudiobook.ensure_audio_generated(book, chapters) 17 end 18 19 {:ok, 20 socket 21 |> assign( 22 active_nav: :library, 23 task_count: active_task_count(), 24 book: book, 25 chapters: chapters, 26 progress: progress, 27 audio_map: audio_map, 28 models: models, 29 selected_model: default_model(book, models), 30 selected_voice: default_voice(book, models), 31 page_title: book.title 32 )} 33 end 34 35 @impl true 36 def handle_event("delete_book", _params, socket) do 37 ReadaloudLibrary.delete_book(socket.assigns.book) 38 {:noreply, push_navigate(socket, to: ~p"/")} 39 end 40 41 @impl true 42 def handle_event("activate_audio", _params, socket) do 43 book = socket.assigns.book 44 model = socket.assigns.selected_model 45 voice = socket.assigns.selected_voice 46 chapters = socket.assigns.chapters 47 48 case ReadaloudLibrary.update_book(book, %{ 49 audio_preferences: %{"model" => model, "voice" => voice} 50 }) do 51 {:ok, book} -> 52 ReadaloudAudiobook.ensure_audio_generated(book, chapters) 53 54 {:noreply, 55 socket 56 |> assign( 57 book: book, 58 audio_map: build_audio_map(chapters, book) 59 )} 60 61 {:error, _changeset} -> 62 {:noreply, put_flash(socket, :error, "Failed to activate audio")} 63 end 64 end 65 66 @impl true 67 def handle_event("update_audio_settings", %{"model" => model, "voice" => voice}, socket) do 68 book = socket.assigns.book 69 chapters = socket.assigns.chapters 70 71 case ReadaloudLibrary.update_book(book, %{ 72 audio_preferences: %{"model" => model, "voice" => voice} 73 }) do 74 {:ok, book} -> 75 ReadaloudAudiobook.ensure_audio_generated(book, chapters) 76 77 {:noreply, 78 socket 79 |> assign( 80 book: book, 81 selected_model: model, 82 selected_voice: voice, 83 audio_map: build_audio_map(chapters, book) 84 )} 85 86 {:error, _changeset} -> 87 {:noreply, put_flash(socket, :error, "Failed to update audio settings")} 88 end 89 end 90 91 @impl true 92 def handle_event("select_model", %{"model" => model_id}, socket) do 93 model = Enum.find(socket.assigns.models, &(&1[:id] == model_id)) 94 voice = if model, do: List.first(model[:voices] || []), else: socket.assigns.selected_voice 95 {:noreply, assign(socket, selected_model: model_id, selected_voice: voice)} 96 end 97 98 @impl true 99 def handle_event("select_voice", %{"voice" => voice}, socket) do 100 {:noreply, assign(socket, selected_voice: voice)} 101 end 102 103 @impl true 104 def handle_info({:task_updated, task}, socket) do 105 book = socket.assigns.book 106 chapters = socket.assigns.chapters 107 108 if task.status == "completed" do 109 ReadaloudAudiobook.ensure_audio_generated(book, chapters) 110 end 111 112 {:noreply, assign(socket, audio_map: build_audio_map(chapters, book))} 113 end 114 115 @impl true 116 def render(assigns) do 117 ~H""" 118
119 <%!-- Back link --%> 120 <.link 121 navigate={~p"/"} 122 class="flex items-center gap-1 text-sm text-base-content/60 hover:text-base-content mb-6" 123 > 124 <.icon name="hero-arrow-left-mini" class="w-4 h-4" /> Back to Library 125 126 127 <%!-- Book header --%> 128

{@book.title}

142 <%= if @book.audio_preferences do %> 143

{@book.author}

200 {audio_count(@audio_map)}/{length(@chapters)} chapters ready · {@book.audio_preferences[ 201 "model" 202 ]} / {@book.audio_preferences["voice"]} 203

206 <.link navigate={resume_path(@book, @progress, @chapters)} class="btn btn-primary btn-sm"> 207 Continue Reading 208 209 <%= if !@book.audio_preferences do %> 210
211
212 Set up audio 213

255
256 <% end %> 257 264

267 268 <%!-- Chapter list --%> 269

277 {ch.number} 278 <.link 279 navigate={~p"/books/#{@book.id}/read/#{ch.id}"} 280 class="flex-1 text-sm hover:text-primary" 281 > 282 {ch.title || "Chapter #{ch.number}"} 283 284 <%= case Map.get(@audio_map, ch.id) do %> 285 <% {:ready, _} -> %> 286 {audio_duration(@audio_map, ch.id)} 287 <% {:stale, _} -> %> 288 {audio_duration(@audio_map, ch.id)} 289 <% {:generating, _} -> %> 290 291 {audio_duration(@audio_map, ch.id)} 292 293 <% {:queued, _} -> %> 294 {audio_duration(@audio_map, ch.id)} 295 <% :processing -> %> 296 generating... 297 <% :queued -> %> 298 queued 299 <% :failed -> %> 300 failed 301 <% :skipped -> %> 302 skipped 303 <% _ -> %> 304 <% end %> 305 306 CURRENT 307 308
309
310
311 """ 312 end 313 314 # --- Private helpers --- 315 316 defp build_audio_map(chapters, book) do 317 chapter_ids = Enum.map(chapters, & &1.id) 318 audios = ReadaloudAudiobook.list_chapter_audio_for_chapters(chapter_ids) 319 tasks = ReadaloudAudiobook.list_tasks_for_chapters(chapter_ids) 320 321 model = get_in(book.audio_preferences || %{}, ["model"]) 322 voice = get_in(book.audio_preferences || %{}, ["voice"]) 323 324 audio_by_chapter = Map.new(audios, &{&1.chapter_id, &1}) 325 326 # Active tasks indexed by chapter 327 active_by_chapter = 328 tasks 329 |> Enum.filter(&(&1.status in ["pending", "processing"])) 330 |> Map.new(&{&1.chapter_id, &1}) 331 332 failed_by_chapter = ReadaloudAudiobook.failed_tasks_by_chapter(tasks, model, voice) 333 334 Map.new(chapter_ids, fn id -> 335 audio = Map.get(audio_by_chapter, id) 336 active_task = Map.get(active_by_chapter, id) 337 failed_task = Map.get(failed_by_chapter, id) 338 audio_matches = audio != nil && audio.model == model && audio.voice == voice 339 340 cond do 341 # Priority 1: Active task exists 342 active_task != nil && active_task.status == "processing" && audio != nil && !audio_matches -> 343 {id, {:generating, audio.duration_seconds}} 344 345 active_task != nil && active_task.status == "pending" && audio != nil && !audio_matches -> 346 {id, {:queued, audio.duration_seconds}} 347 348 active_task != nil && active_task.status == "processing" -> 349 {id, :processing} 350 351 active_task != nil && active_task.status == "pending" -> 352 {id, :queued} 353 354 # Priority 2: Audio matches profile 355 audio_matches -> 356 {id, {:ready, audio.duration_seconds}} 357 358 # Priority 3: Stale audio, no active task 359 audio != nil && !audio_matches -> 360 {id, {:stale, audio.duration_seconds}} 361 362 # Priority 4-5: Failed tasks (matching profile only) 363 failed_task != nil && failed_task.attempt_number >= 3 -> 364 {id, :skipped} 365 366 failed_task != nil -> 367 {id, :failed} 368 369 # Priority 6: Nothing 370 true -> 371 {id, nil} 372 end 373 end) 374 end 375 376 defp current_chapter_number(nil, _chapters), do: 1 377 378 defp current_chapter_number(progress, chapters) do 379 case Enum.find(chapters, &(&1.id == progress.current_chapter_id)) do 380 nil -> 1 381 ch -> ch.number 382 end 383 end 384 385 defp current?(_chapter, nil), do: false 386 defp current?(chapter, progress), do: chapter.id == progress.current_chapter_id 387 388 defp resume_path(book, nil, chapters) do 389 case chapters do 390 [first | _] -> ~p"/books/#{book.id}/read/#{first.id}" 391 [] -> ~p"/books/#{book.id}" 392 end 393 end 394 395 defp resume_path(book, %{current_chapter_id: nil}, _chapters), do: ~p"/books/#{book.id}" 396 397 defp resume_path(book, progress, _chapters), 398 do: ~p"/books/#{book.id}/read/#{progress.current_chapter_id}" 399 400 defp progress_count(nil, _book, _chapters), do: 0 401 402 defp progress_count(progress, _book, chapters) do 403 current_chapter_number(progress, chapters) 404 end 405 406 defp audio_count(audio_map), do: Enum.count(audio_map, fn {_, v} -> match?({:ready, _}, v) end) 407 408 defp audio_duration(audio_map, chapter_id) do 409 case Map.get(audio_map, chapter_id) do 410 {state, seconds} 411 when state in [:ready, :stale, :generating, :queued] and is_number(seconds) and seconds > 0 -> 412 mins = trunc(seconds / 60) 413 secs = trunc(rem(trunc(seconds), 60)) 414 "#{mins}:#{String.pad_leading("#{secs}", 2, "0")}" 415 416 _ -> 417 nil 418 end 419 end 420 421 defp cover_url(book) do 422 if book.cover_path && book.cover_path != "" && File.exists?(book.cover_path) do 423 "/api/books/#{book.id}/cover" 424 else 425 nil 426 end 427 end 428 429 defp gradient_style(book) do 430 CoverResolver.gradient_placeholder(book.title) 431 end 432 end 433

defmodule ReadaloudWebWeb.BookLive do use ReadaloudWebWeb, :live_view alias ReadaloudImporter.CoverResolver @impl true def mount(%{"id" => id}, _session, socket) do book = ReadaloudLibrary.get_book!(String.to_integer(id)) chapters = ReadaloudLibrary.list_chapters(book.id) progress = ReadaloudReader.get_progress(book.id) audio_map = build_audio_map(chapters, book) models = fetch_models() if connected?(socket) do Phoenix.PubSub.subscribe(ReadaloudWeb.PubSub, "tasks:audiobook:#{book.id}") ReadaloudAudiobook.ensure_audio_generated(book, chapters) end {:ok, socket |> assign( active_nav: :library, task_count: active_task_count(), book: book, chapters: chapters, progress: progress, audio_map: audio_map, models: models, selected_model: default_model(book, models), selected_voice: default_voice(book, models), page_title: book.title )} end @impl true def handle_event("delete_book", _params, socket) do ReadaloudLibrary.delete_book(socket.assigns.book) {:noreply, push_navigate(socket, to: ~p"/")} end @impl true def handle_event("activate_audio", _params, socket) do book = socket.assigns.book model = socket.assigns.selected_model voice = socket.assigns.selected_voice chapters = socket.assigns.chapters case ReadaloudLibrary.update_book(book, %{ audio_preferences: %{"model" => model, "voice" => voice} }) do {:ok, book} -> ReadaloudAudiobook.ensure_audio_generated(book, chapters) {:noreply, socket |> assign( book: book, audio_map: build_audio_map(chapters, book) )} {:error, _changeset} -> {:noreply, put_flash(socket, :error, "Failed to activate audio")} end end @impl true def handle_event("update_audio_settings", %{"model" => model, "voice" => voice}, socket) do book = socket.assigns.book chapters = socket.assigns.chapters case ReadaloudLibrary.update_book(book, %{ audio_preferences: %{"model" => model, "voice" => voice} }) do {:ok, book} -> ReadaloudAudiobook.ensure_audio_generated(book, chapters) {:noreply, socket |> assign( book: book, selected_model: model, selected_voice: voice, audio_map: build_audio_map(chapters, book) )} {:error, _changeset} -> {:noreply, put_flash(socket, :error, "Failed to update audio settings")} end end @impl true def handle_event("select_model", %{"model" => model_id}, socket) do model = Enum.find(socket.assigns.models, &(&1[:id] == model_id)) voice = if model, do: List.first(model[:voices] || []), else: socket.assigns.selected_voice {:noreply, assign(socket, selected_model: model_id, selected_voice: voice)} end @impl true def handle_event("select_voice", %{"voice" => voice}, socket) do {:noreply, assign(socket, selected_voice: voice)} end @impl true def handle_info({:task_updated, task}, socket) do book = socket.assigns.book chapters = socket.assigns.chapters if task.status == "completed" do ReadaloudAudiobook.ensure_audio_generated(book, chapters) end {:noreply, assign(socket, audio_map: build_audio_map(chapters, book))} end @impl true def render(assigns) do ~H"""
<%!-- Back link --%> <.link navigate={~p"/"} class="flex items-center gap-1 text-sm text-base-content/60 hover:text-base-content mb-6" > <.icon name="hero-arrow-left-mini" class="w-4 h-4" /> Back to Library <%!-- Book header --%>

{@book.title}

<%= if @book.audio_preferences do %>
<.icon name="hero-cog-6-tooth" class="w-4 h-4" />

{audio_count(@audio_map)}/{length(@chapters)} chapters ready

<% end %>

{@book.author}

{length(@chapters)} chapters {progress_count(@progress, @book, @chapters)}/{length(@chapters)} read <%= if @book.audio_preferences do %> {audio_count(@audio_map)}/{length(@chapters)} audio <% end %>
<%= if @book.audio_preferences do %>

{audio_count(@audio_map)}/{length(@chapters)} chapters ready · {@book.audio_preferences[ "model" ]} / {@book.audio_preferences["voice"]}

<% end %>
<.link navigate={resume_path(@book, @progress, @chapters)} class="btn btn-primary btn-sm"> Continue Reading <%= if !@book.audio_preferences do %>
Set up audio
<% end %>
<%!-- Chapter list --%>
{ch.number} <.link navigate={~p"/books/#{@book.id}/read/#{ch.id}"} class="flex-1 text-sm hover:text-primary" > {ch.title || "Chapter #{ch.number}"} <%= case Map.get(@audio_map, ch.id) do %> <% {:ready, _} -> %> {audio_duration(@audio_map, ch.id)} <% {:stale, _} -> %> {audio_duration(@audio_map, ch.id)} <% {:generating, _} -> %> {audio_duration(@audio_map, ch.id)} <% {:queued, _} -> %> {audio_duration(@audio_map, ch.id)} <% :processing -> %> generating... <% :queued -> %> queued <% :failed -> %> failed <% :skipped -> %> skipped <% _ -> %> <% end %> CURRENT
""" end # --- Private helpers --- defp build_audio_map(chapters, book) do chapter_ids = Enum.map(chapters, & &1.id) audios = ReadaloudAudiobook.list_chapter_audio_for_chapters(chapter_ids) tasks = ReadaloudAudiobook.list_tasks_for_chapters(chapter_ids) model = get_in(book.audio_preferences || %{}, ["model"]) voice = get_in(book.audio_preferences || %{}, ["voice"]) audio_by_chapter = Map.new(audios, &{&1.chapter_id, &1}) # Active tasks indexed by chapter active_by_chapter = tasks |> Enum.filter(&(&1.status in ["pending", "processing"])) |> Map.new(&{&1.chapter_id, &1}) failed_by_chapter = ReadaloudAudiobook.failed_tasks_by_chapter(tasks, model, voice) Map.new(chapter_ids, fn id -> audio = Map.get(audio_by_chapter, id) active_task = Map.get(active_by_chapter, id) failed_task = Map.get(failed_by_chapter, id) audio_matches = audio != nil && audio.model == model && audio.voice == voice cond do # Priority 1: Active task exists active_task != nil && active_task.status == "processing" && audio != nil && !audio_matches -> {id, {:generating, audio.duration_seconds}} active_task != nil && active_task.status == "pending" && audio != nil && !audio_matches -> {id, {:queued, audio.duration_seconds}} active_task != nil && active_task.status == "processing" -> {id, :processing} active_task != nil && active_task.status == "pending" -> {id, :queued} # Priority 2: Audio matches profile audio_matches -> {id, {:ready, audio.duration_seconds}} # Priority 3: Stale audio, no active task audio != nil && !audio_matches -> {id, {:stale, audio.duration_seconds}} # Priority 4-5: Failed tasks (matching profile only) failed_task != nil && failed_task.attempt_number >= 3 -> {id, :skipped} failed_task != nil -> {id, :failed} # Priority 6: Nothing true -> {id, nil} end end) end defp current_chapter_number(nil, _chapters), do: 1 defp current_chapter_number(progress, chapters) do case Enum.find(chapters, &(&1.id == progress.current_chapter_id)) do nil -> 1 ch -> ch.number end end defp current?(_chapter, nil), do: false defp current?(chapter, progress), do: chapter.id == progress.current_chapter_id defp resume_path(book, nil, chapters) do case chapters do [first | _] -> ~p"/books/#{book.id}/read/#{first.id}" [] -> ~p"/books/#{book.id}" end end defp resume_path(book, %{current_chapter_id: nil}, _chapters), do: ~p"/books/#{book.id}" defp resume_path(book, progress, _chapters), do: ~p"/books/#{book.id}/read/#{progress.current_chapter_id}" defp progress_count(nil, _book, _chapters), do: 0 defp progress_count(progress, _book, chapters) do current_chapter_number(progress, chapters) end defp audio_count(audio_map), do: Enum.count(audio_map, fn {_, v} -> match?({:ready, _}, v) end) defp audio_duration(audio_map, chapter_id) do case Map.get(audio_map, chapter_id) do {state, seconds} when state in [:ready, :stale, :generating, :queued] and is_number(seconds) and seconds > 0 -> mins = trunc(seconds / 60) secs = trunc(rem(trunc(seconds), 60)) "#{mins}:#{String.pad_leading("#{secs}", 2, "0")}" _ -> nil end end defp cover_url(book) do if book.cover_path && book.cover_path != "" && File.exists?(book.cover_path) do "/api/books/#{book.id}/cover" else nil end end defp gradient_style(book) do CoverResolver.gradient_placeholder(book.title) end end

defmodule ReadaloudWebWeb.LibraryLive do use ReadaloudWebWeb, :live_view alias ReadaloudImporter.CoverResolver @impl true def mount(_params, _session, socket) do if connected?(socket) do Phoenix.PubSub.subscribe(ReadaloudWeb.PubSub, "tasks:import") end books = ReadaloudLibrary.list_books_sorted("recent") progress_map = build_progress_map(books) {:ok, socket |> assign( active_nav: :library, task_count: active_task_count(), books: books, progress_map: progress_map, search: "", sort: "recent", page_title: "Library" ) |> allow_upload(:book_file, accept: ~w(.epub .pdf), max_entries: 1, max_file_size: 100_000_000 )} end @impl true def handle_event("search", %{"search" => query}, socket) do books = if query == "" do ReadaloudLibrary.list_books_sorted(socket.assigns.sort) else ReadaloudLibrary.search_books(query) end {:noreply, assign(socket, books: books, search: query, progress_map: build_progress_map(books))} end @impl true def handle_event("sort", %{"sort" => sort_by}, socket) do books = ReadaloudLibrary.list_books_sorted(sort_by) {:noreply, socket |> assign(books: books, sort: sort_by, progress_map: build_progress_map(books)) |> push_event("persist_sort", %{sort: sort_by})} end # Existing upload handlers preserved from original implementation @impl true def handle_event("validate", _params, socket) do {:noreply, socket} end @impl true def handle_event("import", _params, socket) do [{file_path, file_type}] = consume_uploaded_entries(socket, :book_file, fn %{path: path}, entry -> ext = Path.extname(entry.client_name) |> String.trim_leading(".") dest = Path.join(upload_dir(), entry.client_name) File.mkdir_p!(Path.dirname(dest)) File.cp!(path, dest) {:ok, {dest, ext}} end) case ReadaloudImporter.import_file(file_path, file_type) do {:ok, _task} -> {:noreply, put_flash(socket, :info, "Import started!")} {:error, _} -> {:noreply, put_flash(socket, :error, "Import failed")} end end @impl true def handle_info({:import_completed, _book_id}, socket) do books = ReadaloudLibrary.list_books_sorted(socket.assigns.sort) {:noreply, assign(socket, books: books, progress_map: build_progress_map(books))} end @impl true def render(assigns) do ~H"""
<%!-- Header with search and controls --%>

Library

<%!-- Search --%>
<%!-- Sort --%>
<%!-- Upload drop zone --%>
<.live_file_input upload={@uploads.book_file} class="file-input file-input-bordered file-input-sm" />

Drop an EPUB or PDF here, or click to browse

<%!-- Upload progress --%>
{entry.client_name}

{humanize_upload_error(err)}

<%!-- Drop overlay --%>
Drop file to upload
<%!-- Empty state --%>
<.icon name="hero-book-open" class="size-12 mx-auto mb-3 opacity-30" />

No books yet

Import an EPUB or PDF to get started

<%!-- Book grid --%>
<.link navigate={resume_path(book, @progress_map)} class="block aspect-[3/4] rounded-lg overflow-hidden relative shadow-md hover:shadow-xl transition-shadow" > <%!-- Cover image or gradient --%>
<%!-- Bottom gradient overlay for text --%>
<%!-- Status badge --%> <%= case status_badge(book, Map.get(@progress_map, book.id)) do %> <% :done -> %> Done <% :new -> %> New <% {:progress, read, total} -> %> {read}/{total} <% _ -> %> <% end %> <%!-- Title and author --%>

{book.title}

{book.author}

<%!-- Info button --%> <.link navigate={~p"/books/#{book.id}"} class="absolute top-2 left-2 btn btn-circle btn-xs btn-ghost bg-black/30 text-white opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100 transition-opacity" title="Book details" > <.icon name="hero-information-circle-mini" class="size-4" />
""" end defp build_progress_map(books) do book_ids = Enum.map(books, & &1.id) ReadaloudReader.list_progress_for_books(book_ids) |> Enum.map(fn p -> {p.book_id, p} end) |> Map.new() end defp resume_path(book, progress_map) do case Map.get(progress_map, book.id) do %{current_chapter_id: ch_id} when not is_nil(ch_id) -> ~p"/books/#{book.id}/read/#{ch_id}" _ -> chapters = ReadaloudLibrary.list_chapters(book.id) case chapters do [first | _] -> ~p"/books/#{book.id}/read/#{first.id}" [] -> ~p"/books/#{book.id}" end end end defp cover_url(book) do if book.cover_path && book.cover_path != "" && File.exists?(book.cover_path) do "/api/books/#{book.id}/cover" else nil end end defp gradient_style(book) do CoverResolver.gradient_placeholder(book.title) end defp status_badge(book, progress) do total = book.total_chapters || 0 read = progress_chapter_count(progress, book) new_cutoff = DateTime.add(DateTime.utc_now(), -7, :day) cond do read > 0 and read >= total -> :done read == 0 and NaiveDateTime.compare(book.inserted_at, DateTime.to_naive(new_cutoff)) == :gt -> :new read > 0 -> {:progress, read, total} true -> nil end end defp progress_chapter_count(nil, _book), do: 0 defp progress_chapter_count(progress, book) do case progress.current_chapter_id do nil -> 0 ch_id -> chapters = ReadaloudLibrary.list_chapters(book.id) current = Enum.find(chapters, &(&1.id == ch_id)) if current, do: current.number, else: 0 end end defp upload_dir do dir = Path.join(System.get_env("STORAGE_PATH", "priv/static/files"), "uploads") File.mkdir_p!(dir) dir end defp humanize_upload_error(:too_large), do: "File is too large (max 100MB)" defp humanize_upload_error(:not_accepted), do: "Only EPUB and PDF files are accepted" defp humanize_upload_error(:too_many_files), do: "Only one file at a time" defp humanize_upload_error(err), do: "Upload error: #{inspect(err)}" end

# Checkpoint 1 — Inventory

## 1. One-sentence summary

`ReaderLive` renders a chapter with a top-mounted floating pill that navigates Library / prev chapter / chapter-strip / next / settings, and `BookLive` renders an unfiltered chapter list where each row is decorated by re-deriving status from `progress.current_chapter_id` inline. Neither the pill nor the book screen has a concept named "back to this book" or "read chapter."

## 2. Values that travel together without a shared type (Move 2)

**A. Chapter read-state.** The triple `{chapter, progress, chapters}` keeps reappearing to compute "is this chapter read / current / unread," with three inconsistent shapes:

- `book_live.ex:385-386` — `current?(chapter, progress)` returns a bool for *only* the single current chapter.
- `book_live.ex:400-404` — `progress_count(progress, _book, chapters)` returns the count of read chapters as `current.number`.
- `library_live.ex:292-303` — `progress_chapter_count(progress, book)` does the same calculation under a different name and re-queries `list_chapters/1` from the DB inside a render loop.

There is **no** `read?(chapter, progress)` predicate anywhere — yet "chapters before the current one are read" is exactly the implicit rule the badges and counts depend on. This is the concept the new filter needs in order to exist.

**B. Pill destinations.** The pill at `reader_live.ex:243-280` mixes one external link (Library, line 251), two scoped buttons (prev/next), one popover toggle (chapter strip), and one settings toggle. "Where the pill can take you" is implicit — every destination is hard-coded, none is centralized, and the per-book screen is absent from the set.

## 3. Classes that are glorified forwarders (Move 3)

None in the targeted code. `prev_chapter/2`, `next_chapter/2`, `chapter_index/2` (`reader_live.ex:689-701`) are 2-line cursor helpers — fine as-is.

## 4. Dependency direction (Move 4)

In scope, there's no inversion to flip. The pill is internal to `ReaderLive`; both LiveViews depend on `ReadaloudReader` (downward) and `ReadaloudLibrary` (downward). No framework-points-at-consumer arrows.

## 5. Unused dependencies (Move 6)

None in scope.

## 6. Round-trip values (Move 9)

None in scope.

## 7. `public` symbols with no cross-module caller (Move 7, surface)

Both LiveViews keep helpers `defp` correctly. Nothing leaking.

## 8. Mutable collections that could be immutable (Move 9)

N/A — Elixir defaults are already immutable.

## 9. Concrete pain in scope of the user's two complaints

- **Pill, missing destination:** the pill's only "out" link is `~p"/"` (Library). The book detail page is reachable from `BookLive`'s back-link but unreachable from the pill — the only intermediate "table of contents" surface in the app is skipped. *(Symptom of Move 2-B.)*
- **Book screen, no filter mechanism:** `book_live.ex:269-309` `<div :for={ch <- @chapters}>` renders the full set unconditionally. There is no assign for filter state and no predicate to filter by. *(Symptom of Move 2-A.)*
- **Concept duplication tax:** `progress_count/3` (book_live) and `progress_chapter_count/2` (library_live) are two copies of the same calculation under different names; the second one re-queries the DB inside a loop. The new filter would be a third call site for the same nameless concept unless it gets a name first.

## The itch I can already name

The two complaints look unrelated, but they share one root: **chapter status has no first-class name**. The pill says "go to chapter N / total N" but doesn't know the difference between "where the user is" and "where the user has been." The book screen lists chapters without distinguishing read from unread. Until `:read | :current | :unread` is a named predicate (or a small status type), the back-button and the filter are both bolted-on UI without a data spine.

---

**Stop. Wait for approval.**

Reply with one of:
- "Proceed to Checkpoint 2" — I'll draft 2-3 candidate call sites for both the pill destinations and the chapter-status predicate.
- "Scope it to just the filter / just the pill" — narrow the next checkpoint.
- "Pick a different target" — re-aim before investing more.

content.text:
# Checkpoint 2 — Call site I'll draft candidate call sites for both APIs the user complaints touch, then pick. --- ## API 1 — Chapter status (powers the hide-read filter) ### Candidate A — three predicates ```elixir # helpers read?(ch, @progress) # → bool current?(ch, @progress) # → bool unread?(ch, @progress) # → bool ``` ```heex
CURRENT ✓
``` **Awkward:** every call site has to `@progress` again, three predicates instead of one named concept. Easy to forget the `unread?` branch when adding a fourth state later. Doesn't reduce the duplicate-DB-query problem in `library_live.progress_chapter_count/2`. ### Candidate B — single function returning a tagged status ```elixir chapter_status(ch, progress) # → :read | :current | :unread ``` ```heex
<%= case chapter_status(ch, @progress) do %> <% :current -> %>CURRENT <% :read -> %>✓ <% :unread -> %> <% end %>
``` **Awkward:** `chapter_status` is recomputed twice per row (filter + render). A `:let` on `:for` would solve it but Phoenix templates don't support that. Move 7 OK because `case` exhaustiveness is enforced by tests not the compiler — but adding a fourth state forces every `case` to fail visibly. ### Candidate C — annotate at the boundary, pattern-match in the loop *(picked)* ```elixir # In mount + every place chapters are assigned: chapters = ReadaloudReader.annotate_chapters(chapters, progress) # → [%{chapter: %Chapter{}, status: :read | :current | :unread}, ...] ``` ```heex <%!-- header counts (replaces progress_count/3 + progress_chapter_count/2) --%> {Enum.count(@chapters, & &1.status == :read)}/{length(@chapters)} read <%!-- list --%>
{ch.number} <.link navigate={~p"/books/#{@book.id}/read/#{ch.id}"}>{ch.title} CURRENT ✓
``` **Justification against the moves:** - Names the implicit concept once (Move 2). The triple `{chapter, progress, chapters}` becomes a `%{chapter, status}` everywhere it's currently re-derived. - Kills the `progress_count/3` ↔ `progress_chapter_count/2` duplication and the per-book `list_chapters/1` re-query in `library_live.ex:300` (Move 6, dead-weight dependency on chapters list inside the helper). - Lets the filter call site collapse to `:if={s != :read or not @hide_read}` — wrong uses become hard to express (Move 7). - Annotation at the boundary (Move 5: convert at the edge); the LiveView never sees raw `progress` again for status purposes. - Single canonical place to extend: add `:in_progress` later → one function, every call site forced to handle it (case exhaustiveness). **The one tradeoff I'm accepting:** chapters become `%{chapter:, status:}` rather than raw `%Chapter{}`. Templates must destructure. Worth it — that's exactly what makes the concept visible. --- ## API 2 — Pill back-to-book navigation ### Candidate A — single new icon link ```heex <.link navigate={~p"/"}> <.link navigate={~p"/books/#{@book.id}"}> ``` **Awkward:** two icons next to each other with no visual relationship. User has to know the second one is "the book." Implicit hierarchy. ### Candidate B — hierarchical breadcrumb *(picked)* ```heex <%!-- pill --%> <.link navigate={~p"/"} title="Library"> / <.link navigate={~p"/books/#{@book.id}"} title={@book.title}> {truncate(@book.title, 18)} / ``` **Justification against the moves:** - Move 1 (call site first): the user reads the pill left-to-right and sees the entire navigation hierarchy — Library, this book, this chapter. "Back to the book" is *spatially* where you'd expect it. The implicit concept "where can I escape to" becomes visible (Move 2). - Move 7 (discoverable): the title is the link label, not an unmarked icon — autocomplete-equivalent for a UI surface. - Width: book title truncated at ~18 chars is the only new aesthetic constraint. Acceptable cost for the orientation gain. **The tradeoff:** pill gets ~80px wider. On very narrow viewports the title may need to be a 📖 icon-only fallback. Worth a single CSS rule. ### Candidate C — pluralized destinations component ```heex <.pill_destinations crumbs={[ {:library, ~p"/"}, {:book, ~p"/books/#{@book.id}", @book.title} ]} /> ``` **Awkward:** premature seam. There's exactly one caller (the reader pill). Move 8: hard-code, don't speculate. Reject. --- ## Summary - **Chapter status:** Candidate C — annotated `[%{chapter, status}]` list at the boundary, pattern-match in templates. Eliminates two duplicates and gives the filter a 1-character call site. - **Pill destinations:** Candidate B — explicit `Library / Book Title / chapter nav` breadcrumb in the pill itself. These two changes are independent — either can ship without the other — but they share the same diagnosis: the implicit hierarchy needs a name. --- **Stop. Wait for approval.** Reply with one of: - "Proceed to Checkpoint 3" with both call sites accepted - "Use A/B/C for status / pill" if you want to swap a choice - "Reject the annotated list" if you'd rather keep raw `%Chapter{}` and use predicates
